ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for diet root beer; mapped to broader carbonated soft drink class (FOODON_03301589).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated diet root beer code; A0CKW (diet/light carbonated soft drinks) is the closest defensible parent-level match in the FoodEx2 hierarchy for artificially sweetened carbonated beverages.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for diet root beer; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: natural and artificial root beer flavours

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single serving of diet root beer is one 355 mL (12 fl oz) can, which contains essentially zero calories, zero sugar, and approximately 99 mg of sodium.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, carbonated, low calorie, other than cola or pepper, without caffeine — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 14148)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for low-calorie non-cola carbonated beverage used as the closest approved-source match for diet root beer. Singapore HPB database values for energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, and iron are consistent with USDA values and are preserved from the input record. Sodium value of 28 mg/100g from HPB is retained; USDA reports approximately 12–14 mg/100g for comparable products — HPB value accepted as product-specific. All zero macros confirmed by both HPB and USDA for this beverage category.

gi_source.title: N/A — Diet root beer contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g/100g); glycemic index is not applicable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Diet root beer contains 0 g carbohydrates per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable for foods with negligible carbohydrate content. No GI value is assigned. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. University of Sydney GI Database consulted; no entry exists for diet root beer as it contains no fermentable carbohydrates. GI is set to null and gi_category to not_applicable per Project Veritas v2 rules for negligible-carbohydrate foods.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (28 mg/100g); no sugar; no alcohol
hypertension.guidance: Diet root beer contains only 28 mg sodium per 100 mL and zero sugar, placing it well below hypertension-relevant thresholds; however, individuals with hypertension should moderate consumption due to potential effects of artificial sweeteners (aspartame, acesulfame-K) on blood pressure variability reported in some observational studies, and should prioritise water as their primary hydration source.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero sugar and zero carbohydrates; no direct glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Diet root beer contains 0 g sugar and 0 g carbohydrates per 100 mL and does not trigger a glycaemic response; however, people with type 2 diabetes should be aware that chronic heavy consumption of artificial sweeteners may be associated with altered gut microbiome composition and insulin sensitivity in some research, and moderate intake is advised alongside regular blood glucose monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero calories, zero fat, zero sugar; low sodium; no alcohol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Diet root beer is calorie-free, fat-free, and sugar-free with low sodium (28 mg/100 mL), making it unlikely to directly worsen cardiovascular risk factors; nonetheless, regular consumption of artificially sweetened beverages has been associated in some prospective cohort studies with increased cardiovascular event risk, and water or unsweetened beverages remain the preferred choice for cardiovascular health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: No anti-inflammatory nutrients; phosphoric acid; artificial sweeteners
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Diet root beer provides no omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols, antioxidants, or dietary fibre that would benefit rheumatoid arthritis management; phosphoric acid may adversely affect bone mineral density in patients on long-term corticosteroids, and green tea, water, or polyphenol-rich beverages are preferred alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Diet root beer is a sugar-free, zero-calorie carbonated soft drink flavoured with natural and artificial root beer flavourings — typically a blend of vanilla, wintergreen, anise, and other botanical extracts — and sweetened with artificial sweeteners such as aspartame and acesulfame potassium instead of sugar. It is an ultra-processed beverage produced by carbonating water, adding flavour compounds, caramel colour for appearance, phosphoric acid for tartness, and preservatives such as sodium benzoate. The taste closely mimics traditional root beer — sweet, slightly herbal, and effervescent — but without the caloric load of sugar-sweetened versions. Diet root beer contains essentially zero energy (0 kcal/100 mL), zero protein, zero fat, zero carbohydrates, and zero sugar per 100 mL, with a modest sodium content of 28 mg/100 mL sourced from the Singapore HPB database. It is widely consumed as a low-calorie alternative to regular root beer and is popular in the United States and increasingly available in Singapore and Southeast Asia. From a clinical nutrition perspective, diet root beer does not raise blood glucose and is not contraindicated for type 2 diabetes or hypertension on the basis of its macronutrient profile alone; however, the long-term health effects of artificial sweeteners remain an active area of research. It is not a source of any beneficial micronutrients, antioxidants, or anti-inflammatory compounds, and water remains the preferred primary hydration source for all health conditions. Individuals with phenylketonuria (PKU) must avoid products containing aspartame due to the phenylalanine content.
common_user_queries: Is diet root beer safe for diabetics?
How many calories are in diet root beer?
Does diet root beer have sugar?
Is diet root beer good for weight loss?
Can I drink diet root beer if I have high blood pressure?
What sweeteners are in diet root beer?
Does diet root beer have caffeine?
What is the sodium content of diet root beer?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values (all zero except sodium at 28 mg/100g) are sourced from the Singapore HPB database with 0.95 confidence and are consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for low-calorie carbonated soft drinks. The zero-macro profile is characteristic and well-documented for artificially sweetened diet beverages. No estimation was required for core values.
notes: Zero-macro justification: protein_g is confirmed zero — diet root beer is an artificially sweetened carbonated water-based beverage containing no protein by formulation; confirmed zero per Singapore HPB database and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 14148). fat_g is confirmed zero — no fat-containing ingredients are present in diet root beer formulation; confirmed zero per Singapore HPB database and USDA FoodData Central. carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero — all sweetness is provided by non-caloric artificial sweeteners (aspartame, acesulfame-K) with no fermentable carbohydrates; confirmed zero per Singapore HPB database and USDA FoodData Central. energy_kcal is confirmed zero as a direct consequence of zero protein, fat, and carbohydrate content. Negative sentinel values: potassium_mg set to -1 as detailed mineral data for this specific product formulation is not available in the Singapore HPB database; USDA SR Legacy reports approximately 1 mg/100g for comparable products but this is below reliable detection threshold for the HPB record. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: B-vitamin and trace mineral data for diet root beer are not reported in the Singapore HPB database and are below detection threshold in USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID 14148) for this beverage category. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains 0 g carbohydrates per 100 mL; glycemic_index set to null and gi_category set to not_applicable per Project Veritas v2 rules. GL per serving is 0.0 by definition. Caution: products containing aspartame carry a phenylalanine warning relevant to individuals with phenylketonuria (PKU). Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_03301589 (carbonated soft drink) used as closest_parent class match. FoodEx2 A0CKW used as closest_parent match for diet/light carbonated soft drinks — no dedicated diet root beer code exists in FoodEx2. No SNOMED CT code assigned. gi_source.source_match_type set to 'exact' reflecting that the University of Sydney GI Database was directly consulted and confirmed no applicable GI entry exists for this zero-carbohydrate beverage.
